stem: In a right triangle whose acute angles measure \( 30^{\circ} \) and \( 60^{\circ} \), the side opposite the \( 30^{\circ} \) angle has length \( [[a]] \). What is the length of the side opposite the \( 60^{\circ} \) angle?
solution: In a \( 30^{\circ}\,\text{-}\,60^{\circ}\,\text{-}\,90^{\circ} \) triangle the sides are in the ratio \( 1 : \sqrt{3} : 2 \) opposite the \( 30^{\circ}, 60^{\circ}, 90^{\circ} \) angles. The side opposite \( 60^{\circ} \) is \( \sqrt{3} \) times the side opposite \( 30^{\circ} \), so it equals \( [[a]]\sqrt{3} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This applies the \( 45^{\circ}\,\text{-}\,45^{\circ}\,\text{-}\,90^{\circ} \) ratio \( \sqrt{2} \) instead of \( \sqrt{3} \).
This is the hypotenuse (twice the short leg), not the long leg.
This divides by \( \sqrt{3} \) instead of multiplying by it.
This is half of the correct long leg.
This just repeats the short leg without applying any ratio.
This scales the short leg by 3 rather than by \( \sqrt{3} \).

stem: In a \( 30^{\circ}\text{-}60^{\circ}\text{-}90^{\circ} \) right triangle, the shorter leg has length \( [[a]] \). What is the length of the hypotenuse?
solution: In a \( 30^{\circ}\text{-}60^{\circ}\text{-}90^{\circ} \) triangle the sides are in ratio \( 1:\sqrt{3}:2 \), so the hypotenuse is twice the side opposite \( 30^{\circ} \): \( 2\cdot [[a]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the long leg (opposite \( 60^{\circ} \)), not the hypotenuse.
This applies the \( 45\text{-}45\text{-}90 \) ratio instead of the \( 30\text{-}60\text{-}90 \) ratio.
This halves the short leg instead of doubling it.
This just repeats the short leg without applying any ratio.
This triples the short leg instead of doubling it.
This is half of the correct long leg, not the hypotenuse.

stem: In a \( 30^{\circ}\text{-}60^{\circ}\text{-}90^{\circ} \) right triangle, the side opposite the \( 60^{\circ} \) angle has length \( [[L]] \). What is the length of the side opposite the \( 30^{\circ} \) angle?
solution: Since the side opposite \( 60^{\circ} \) is \( \sqrt{3} \) times the side opposite \( 30^{\circ} \), the short leg is \( \dfrac{[[L]]}{\sqrt{3}} = \dfrac{[[L]]\sqrt{3}}{3} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This multiplies by \( \sqrt{3} \) instead of dividing by it.
This mistakenly treats the long leg as the hypotenuse and halves it.
This applies the \( 45\text{-}45\text{-}90 \) ratio instead of the \( 30\text{-}60\text{-}90 \) ratio.
This doubles the long leg before dividing by \( \sqrt{3} \), an extra factor of 2.
This just repeats the long leg without applying any ratio.
This divides by 3 instead of by \( \sqrt{3} \).

stem: In a \( 30^{\circ}\text{-}60^{\circ}\text{-}90^{\circ} \) right triangle, the hypotenuse has length \( [[H]] \). What is the length of the shorter leg?
solution: In a \( 30^{\circ}\text{-}60^{\circ}\text{-}90^{\circ} \) triangle the hypotenuse is twice the short leg, so the short leg is \( \dfrac{[[H]]}{2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the long leg (opposite \( 60^{\circ} \)), not the short leg.
This applies the \( 45\text{-}45\text{-}90 \) ratio instead of the \( 30\text{-}60\text{-}90 \) ratio.
This doubles the hypotenuse instead of halving it.
This divides by \( \sqrt{3} \) instead of by 2.
This just repeats the hypotenuse without applying any ratio.
This divides by 3 instead of by 2.

stem: In a \( 30^{\circ}\text{-}60^{\circ}\text{-}90^{\circ} \) right triangle, the hypotenuse has length \( [[H]] \). What is the length of the longer leg?
solution: In a \( 30^{\circ}\text{-}60^{\circ}\text{-}90^{\circ} \) triangle, the short leg is \( \frac{H}{2} \) and the long leg is \( \sqrt{3} \) times the short leg, so the long leg is \( \dfrac{[[H]]}{2}\sqrt{3} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the short leg (opposite \( 30^{\circ} \)), not the long leg.
This applies the \( 45\text{-}45\text{-}90 \) ratio instead of the \( 30\text{-}60\text{-}90 \) ratio.
This uses the full hypotenuse instead of the short leg before multiplying by \( \sqrt{3} \).
This divides by \( \sqrt{3} \) instead of multiplying by it.
This just repeats the hypotenuse without applying any ratio.
This uses an extra factor of 2 in the denominator.

stem: In a \( 30^{\circ}\text{-}60^{\circ}\text{-}90^{\circ} \) right triangle, the longer leg has length \( [[L]] \). What is the length of the hypotenuse?
solution: The short leg is \( \dfrac{[[L]]}{\sqrt{3}} \), and the hypotenuse is twice the short leg: \( \dfrac{2\cdot[[L]]}{\sqrt{3}} = \dfrac{2\cdot[[L]]\sqrt{3}}{3} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This mistakes the long leg's ratio for the hypotenuse's, using \( \sqrt{3} \) directly.
This doubles the long leg directly, as if it were the short leg.
This halves the long leg instead of using the \( 1:\sqrt3:2 \) ratio.
This forgets to double after finding the short leg.
This applies the \( 45\text{-}45\text{-}90 \) ratio instead of the \( 30\text{-}60\text{-}90 \) ratio.
This divides instead of finding the hypotenuse via the short leg.

stem: In an isosceles right triangle (a \( 45^{\circ}\text{-}45^{\circ}\text{-}90^{\circ} \) triangle), each leg has length \( [[a]] \). What is the length of the hypotenuse?
solution: In a \( 45^{\circ}\text{-}45^{\circ}\text{-}90^{\circ} \) triangle the sides are in ratio \( 1:1:\sqrt{2} \), so the hypotenuse is \( [[a]]\sqrt{2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This applies the \( 30\text{-}60\text{-}90 \) ratio \( \sqrt{3} \) instead of \( \sqrt{2} \).
This simply doubles the leg instead of multiplying by \( \sqrt{2} \).
This divides by \( \sqrt{2} \) instead of multiplying by it.
This is half of the correct hypotenuse.
This just repeats the leg without applying any ratio.
This scales the leg by 3 rather than by \( \sqrt{2} \).

stem: In an isosceles right triangle (a \( 45^{\circ}\text{-}45^{\circ}\text{-}90^{\circ} \) triangle), the hypotenuse has length \( [[H]] \). What is the length of each leg?
solution: In a \( 45^{\circ}\text{-}45^{\circ}\text{-}90^{\circ} \) triangle each leg is \( \dfrac{\text{hypotenuse}}{\sqrt{2}} \), so the leg is \( \dfrac{[[H]]}{\sqrt{2}} = \dfrac{[[H]]\sqrt{2}}{2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This applies the \( 30\text{-}60\text{-}90 \) ratio \( \sqrt{3} \) instead of \( \sqrt{2} \).
This simply halves the hypotenuse without applying the \( \sqrt{2} \) factor.
This multiplies by \( \sqrt{2} \) instead of dividing by it.
This has an extra factor of 2 in the numerator.
This just repeats the hypotenuse without applying any ratio.
This divides by 3 instead of by \( \sqrt{2} \).

stem: A \( 30^{\circ}\text{-}60^{\circ}\text{-}90^{\circ} \) right triangle has its shortest side (opposite the \( 30^{\circ} \) angle) equal to \( [[a]] \). What is the area of the triangle?
solution: The two legs are \( [[a]] \) (short) and \( [[a]]\sqrt{3} \) (long), and they are perpendicular, so the area is \( \frac{1}{2}\cdot [[a]]\cdot [[a]]\sqrt{3} = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}[[a]]^{2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses the short leg squared with no \( \sqrt{3} \) factor, as if both legs equaled the short leg.
This forgets the \( \sqrt{3} \) factor entirely, halving the short leg squared.
This forgets the \( \frac{1}{2} \) factor in the triangle-area formula.
This uses \( \frac{1}{4} \) instead of \( \frac{1}{2} \) as the area coefficient.
This is off from the correct area by the short leg.
This is off from the correct area by the short leg.

stem: An isosceles right triangle (a \( 45^{\circ}\text{-}45^{\circ}\text{-}90^{\circ} \) triangle) has each leg equal to \( [[a]] \). What is the area of the triangle?
solution: The two legs are equal and perpendicular, each of length \( [[a]] \), so the area is \( \frac{1}{2}\cdot [[a]]\cdot [[a]] = \dfrac{[[a]]^{2}}{2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the \( \frac{1}{2} \) factor in the triangle-area formula.
This mistakenly multiplies in a \( \sqrt{2} \) factor, confusing this with the hypotenuse formula.
This doubles the correct area instead of halving the square of the leg.
This uses \( \frac{1}{4} \) instead of \( \frac{1}{2} \) as the area coefficient.
This is off from the correct area by the leg length.
This is off from the correct area by the leg length.
